mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or the sign of the correlation flips in more than one of three consecutive out-of-sample folds.

mechanism_narrative: When month-over-month PAYEMS growth decelerates below 0.1% after a period of stronger job gains, it signals an imminent labor-market inflection that triggers rapid equity repricing of growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS m/m growth falls below 0.1% after prior 3-month average above 0.2%
failure_condition: In more than 55% of historical trigger events, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive, or the average triggered return is not statistically distinguishable from the unconditional 5-day return distribution (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that is not captured by either series alone, driving sustained S&P 500 gains over a full quarter (60 trading days) as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS+HOUST 3-month growth interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over either series individually (interaction coefficient not significant at p<0.10) in a 60-day forward return regression, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5% while CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated, markets price a higher probability of policy overtightening; further increases in FEDFUNDS combined with sticky CPI in this regime produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as recession risk repricing dominates.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns during triggered periods are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction effect disappears (joint coefficient p > 0.10) once conditioned on FEDFUNDS level alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals a labor-market turning point historically associated with recession onset, causing equity markets to discount forward earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: Following triggered UNRATE increases, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive on average or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year evaluation window, indicating the Sahm-rule-style trigger has lost predictive content.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding real economic capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a full quarter, independent of prevailing monetary or labor conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month INDPRO growth and forward 60-day SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or the sign of the relationship reverses in two consecutive out-of-sample folds.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in housing starts exceeding 10% signals a contraction in interest-rate-sensitive construction demand that historically precedes broader economic slowdowns, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, with the effect strongest when the decline occurs amid elevated financing costs.
regime_hint: housing downturn amid high interest rates
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following triggered HOUST declines are non-negative on average across the historical sample, or the effect is not robust to excluding recession-dated periods.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds 4% year-over-year while the federal funds rate is simultaneously rising, the combined signal of persistent inflation and active tightening amplifies equity volatility and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the very short 5-day horizon as markets reprice near-term rate-hike expectations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change > 4% and FEDFUNDS increased in the last month
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns during triggered joint high-inflation-and-hiking periods are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term loses significance (p > 0.10) relative to either series alone.
